learning: OI walls can break after 3-4 tests in the same session. The 24,000 PE wall (21M OI) held at 9:35, 11:45, 12:15, then broke at 12:40. Repeated testing weakens the wall — each test going slightly lower is the tell.
context: Called sideways (24000-24100). 24000 tested at 24005, 24018, 24013, then broke at 23987. Each bounce was weaker. Pattern: test→bounce 60pts, test→bounce 40pts, test→bounce 20pts, then break.

learning: Sideways range on pre-expiry day held for first 2.5 hours (9:15-11:45), then broke bearish. Morning ranges on expiry-eve days are NOT reliable for the full session — expect afternoon expansion.
context: Range 24005-24120 held perfectly until 12:40 PM, then 24000 wall broke and spot accelerated to 23931. The morning range was correct but had a finite lifetime.

learning: Slow grinding toward a strike with consecutive higher lows over 60+ min = breakout imminent. Don't wait for it to happen — the grind IS the warning signal. Exit proactively when you see 5+ bars of slow grind toward your position.
context: Called bearish, spot was at 23880. Then slowly ground higher: 23880→23900→23916→23930→23943 over 60 min (higher lows each bar). Then gamma squeeze 23943→23998 in one bar. The grind pattern was the breakout signal — not the spike.

learning: Bearish call after gap-up rejection can be correct initially (first hour selloff) but wrong for the session if the gap-down doesn't sustain. A gap-up rejected that consolidates mid-range (not making lower lows) is shifting to bullish/sideways, not confirming bearish.
context: Gap up 23978→24032 rejected, sold to 23852 (bearish confirmed). But then grind back to 23943-23998 range — bears couldn't break 23850 and bulls accumulated. The bearish direction was right for 1 hour, wrong for the day.

learning: Gap-up opens with sustained higher highs for 30 min (no pullback) are bullish — but ALWAYS wait for the first pullback before calling direction. The 50-75% retracement of the first impulse comes within the first hour.
context: NIFTY gapped 23988→24142. First 30 min made higher highs every bar. Then pulled back 75% (24159→24081). The bullish direction was correct but entry at 24142 would have faced 78pt adverse move. Wait for retrace.

learning: Gap-up open=high with immediate selloff does NOT mean bearish for the day. It's just opening volatility. Today opened 24378, dumped to 24295, then ranged sideways for 4 hours before breaking down in the afternoon.
context: Opening bar open=high (24378→24295). Could have called bearish. Instead waited, and market ranged sideways 24295-24378 for 4 hours. The initial selloff was NOT predictive of final direction — only of opening range.

learning: Afternoon range expansion: when spot lingers at lower support for 30+ min making successive lower lows without a V-bounce (unlike morning tests), that's absorption before breakdown. Morning bounces are sharp; afternoon grinds signal exhaustion.
context: Morning: spot touched 24312 at 10:05 and V-bounced to 24340 in 5 min. Afternoon: spot sat at 24327→24317→24311→24304 from 1:00-1:35 PM without any bounce. Then broke 24295 decisively at 1:40 PM. The difference: morning had sharp bounces, afternoon had weak grinds.

learning: OI walls are not absolute caps in strong directional moves. When spot approaches and then breaches a major OI resistance level, it signals the start of a directional move, not a rejection. 24,400 CE had 16.7M OI but was breached at 10:40 IST and market pushed to 24,459.
context: Called sideways (24,340-24,400) at 09:45 based on 16.7M CE OI wall at 24,400. Market broke 24,400 at 10:40 and pushed to 24,459 by 1:20 PM. The wall failed because the bullish momentum was stronger than the OI absorption capacity. OI walls work in rangebound markets, not trending ones.

learning: Gap-up with continuous higher lows from bar 1 is a bullish flag, not sideways consolidation. Don't confuse a bullish pause (staircase of higher lows) with a true range (equal highs and lows). Structure: 24,287→24,359→24,366 by 09:45 = bullish, not sideways.
context: Opened at 24,311 (gap up), then made higher lows every bar: 24,287→24,359→24,366 by 09:45. Called it sideways because it consolidated around 24,376 for 7 bars. Wrong — it was a bullish flag. The higher lows were the tell. Market continued bullish to 24,459. A true sideways range has equal highs/lows, not a staircase.

learning: On gap-day recoveries after multi-hour rally, don't call sideways when price is still making HH/HL pattern. Slow grind up with consolidation pauses = bull trend continuation, not sideways range. A 'double top' that doesn't make lower lows is just a pause before breakout.
context: Gap-down 24027, rallied to 24149 by 11:15. Called sideways (24100-24157) based on 'double top' at 24162 and consolidation. Wrong — market was in slow bull trend, broke out to 24260. The HH/HL structure (24000→24129→24157→24162) was the tell. No lower lows = not sideways. True sideways needs LH/LL reversal structure.
